LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE DEFENDANT HONORWAY INVESTMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PHYSICAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED. NOTWITHSTANDING THE PARTIES' MODIFIED/AGREED NOTICE OF EXAMINATION TERMS, DEFENDANT'S EXAMINER, DR. CASSINI, INSISTED ON AUDIOTAPING HIS EXAMINATION SIMULTANEOUSLY WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S SAME EFFORT. THAT DISPUTED ISSUE IS NOT SIGNIFICANT IN THE CONTEXT OF THE OVERALL FORMAL MEDICAL-EXAMINATION PROCEEDING. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS OF INJURY ARE COMPLEX AND HER MEDICAL TREATMENT IS REFLECTED IN MANY THOUSANDS OF PAGES OF ENTRIES. UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, TO DENY THE MEDICAL EXAMINATION WOULD EXALT PROCESS OVER SUBSTANCE. THE DEFENDANT WOULD BE DENIED THE RIGHT TO OBTAIN MEDICAL EVIDENCE REFUTING PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS OR AGREEING WITH THEM. HAD THE AUDIOTAPING BEEN ALLOWED THERE WOULD BE NO CONCEIVABLE PREJUDICE TO THE PLAINTIFF. IF THE DEFENDANT CHOOSES TO CONTINUE WITH DR. CASSINI AS ITS EXAMINER, SIMULTANEOUS AUDIOTAPING SHALL BE PERMITTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. DEFENDANT MET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THE DEFECT WAS "TRIVIAL" UNDER GOVT. CODE SEC. 830(A). (UNDISPUTED FACT ("UF") NO. 7; DECL. OF MIKE HAASE, 3, EX. A.) THE BURDEN THEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PLAINTIFF MET HER BURDEN BY PRODUCING A ZOOMED-IN PHOTOGRAPH OF THE RAISED BRICK, SHOWING PIECES WERE BROKEN OFF AND JAGGED EDGES WERE PRESENT. (PLANTIFF'S EVIDENCE, EX. 2H.) JAGGED BREAKS ARE AGGRAVATING FACTORS THAT MILITATE AGAINST A FINDING THAT A CONDITION IS TRIVIAL AS A MATTER OF LAW. (FIELDER V. CITY OF GLENDALE ((1977) 71 CAL.APP.3D 719, 726.) THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF MET HER BURDEN ESTABLISHING THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE JAGGED PIECES OF RAISED BRICKS WERE TRIVIAL OR A DANGEROUS CONDITION. TO PREVAIL ON HER SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY, PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS TO ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE DANGEROUS CONDITION. (GOVT. CODE SEC. 835.) DEFENDANT MET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING IT DID NOT HAVE ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE JAGGED PIECES OF THE RAISED BRICKS. (UF NOS. 12, 15, 16.) THE BURDEN AGAIN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT MEET HER BURDEN IN PRODUCING COMPETENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE. PLAINTIFF PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE ABOUT WHEN THE DANGEROUS CONDITION, THE JAGGED BREAK OF THE RAISED BRICKS, FIRST AROSE. THE DECLARATION OF JEFF MASTIN, PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT, LACKS FOUNDATION (EVID. CODE SEC. 403), IS AN UNQUALIFIED EXPERT OPINION (EVID. CODE SEC. 720), AND IS BASED ON IMPROPER MATTER (EVID. CODE SEC. 804) AS TO WHEN THE BREAKING OF THE BRICKS OCCURRED
OBJECTIONS TO PLTFF'S EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION FILED BY DEFENDANT HONORWAY INVESTMENT CORPORATION
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-28-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re DEFENDANT HONORWAY INVESTMENT CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination. Defendant's motion to compel physical examination is granted. Notwithstanding the parties' modified/agreed Notice of Examination terms, defendant's examiner, Dr. Cassini, insisted on audiotaping his examination simultaneously with plaintiff's counsel's same effort. That disputed issue is not significant in the context of the overall formal medical-examination proceeding. Plaintiff's allegations of injury are complex and her medical treatment is reflected in many thousands of pages of entries. Under the circumstances, to deny the medical examination would exalt process over substance. The defendant would be denied the right to obtain medical evidence refuting plaintiff's claims or agreeing with them. Had the audiotaping been allowed there would be no conceivable prejudice to the plaintiff. If the defendant chooses to continue with Dr. Cassini as its examiner, simultaneous audiotaping shall be permitted. (Prevailing party to submit a proposed order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT H.Z. SUMMER OF KBSS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510)867-2000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NEWTON OLDFATHER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4283 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is denied. Defendant met its burden of establishing the defect was "trivial" under Govt. Code sec. 830(a). (Undisputed Fact ("UF") no. 7; Decl. of Mike Haase, 3, Ex. A.) The burden then shifted to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff met her burden by producing a zoomed-in photograph of the raised brick, showing pieces were broken off and jagged edges were present. (Plantiff's Evidence, Ex. 2H.) Jagged breaks are aggravating factors that militate against a finding that a condition is trivial as a matter of law. (Fielder v. City of Glendale ((1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 719, 726.) Therefore, Plaintiff met her burden establishing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the jagged pieces of raised bricks were trivial or a dangerous condition. To prevail on her sole cause of action for dangerous condition of public property, Plaintiff also has to establish that Defendant had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition. (Govt. Code sec. 835.) Defendant met its burden of establishing it did not have actual or constructive notice of the jagged pieces of the raised bricks. (UF nos. 12, 15, 16.) The burden again shifted to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff did not meet her burden in producing competent evidence showing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant had actual or constructive notice. Plaintiff presented no evidence about when the dangerous condition, the jagged break of the raised bricks, first arose. The Declaration of Jeff Mastin, Plaintiff's expert, lacks foundation (Evid. Code sec. 403), is an unqualified expert opinion (Evid. Code sec. 720), and is based on improper matter (Evid. Code sec. 804) as to when the breaking of the bricks occurred. "[I]n determining constructive notice, the method of inspection is secondary; the primary and indispensable element of constructive notice is a showing that the condition existed before the accident'[there must be sufficient evidence to show whether] the condition has existed long enough that it may be inferred that a reasonable inspection would have ascertained its existence." (Strongman v. County of Kern (1967) 255 Cal.App.2d 308, 313.) Further, Plaintiff has not met her burden of showing actual notice because Plaintiff presented no evidence establishing that a public officer had knowledge of the jagged break of the raised bricks, given that there is no evidence whether the jagged break was present in 2008 or 2009. (See State v. Superior Court for San Mateo County (1968) 263 Cal.App.2d 396, 399.) Plaintiff's request to continue the hearing date pursuant to CCP sec. 437c(h) is denied. The motion was originally filed on July 2, 2013. Plaintiff has had three requests to continue the hearing date granted. Plaintiff had ample time to file a motion to compel depositions but failed to do so. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
